Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log from a Procure to Pay P2P process We need to identify anomalous sequences that deviate from the standard P2P process flow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The standard P2P process typically follows these steps in order 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case Deviations from this order might indicate anomalies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Sequence Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case This seems normal and follows the standard order

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Sequence Create Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order Vendor Invoice Received Release Payment Approve Purchase Requisition Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Issue Purchase Order occurs before Approve Purchase Requisition Typically a PO should only be issued after the requisition is approved Issuing a PO without approval is a control issue

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Release Payment occurs before Approve Purchase Requisition and also before Receive Goods and Match Invoice to PO Payment should be released only after invoice matching three way match PO receipt invoice to ensure that payment is for goods actually received and at the correct price Here payment is released before receiving goods and before matching This could lead to paying for undelivered or incorrect goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approve Purchase Requisition appears after payment release which is out of order and defeats the purpose of approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receive Goods occurs after payment release meaning payment was made before receiving goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Match Invoice to PO occurs after payment release so payment was made without matching increasing risk of overpayment or fraud

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Sequence Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Release Payment occurs before Vendor Invoice Received before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice to PO Payment should be released after invoice matching Here payment is released very early right after issuing PO This is problematic because payment is made without an invoice and without receiving goods Possibly indicates advance payment but typically not standard without proper controls

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Vendor Invoice Received after payment release Usually invoice comes before payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receive Goods after payment release Goods not received yet

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Match Invoice to PO after payment release Matching should occur before payment to validate

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Sequence Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Receive Goods Issue Purchase Order Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receive Goods occurs before Issue Purchase Order Typically goods should be received after a purchase order is issued to the vendor Receiving goods before issuing a PO could mean that goods were ordered informally without a PO which violates procurement controls It could lead to unauthorized purchases and difficulties in matching invoices

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Overall these anomalies indicate weaknesses in internal controls which could lead to fraud overpayment payment for undelivered goods or unapproved purchases
